title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Defense · Kubernetes Production Defense Board
summary: Unfamiliar Kubernetes architecture, API, kubectl, Pod, configuration, workload, probe, rollout, network, Gateway, storage, scheduling, scaling, identity, security, observability, packaging, extension, upgrade, and recovery cases requiring deterministic evidence plus explicit live-cluster transfer limits. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…

Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation.
Predict decoding, defaulting, validation, admission, conversion, persistence, and unknown-field behavior from the published schema.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

hints: For Submit the certification defense · Exam K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Submit the certification defense · Exam K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A exam board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

For Submit the certification defense · Exam K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service.
